title: Centella eriantha
taxonomy: Centella eriantha is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, which is commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. This plant is native to Australia and is also found in other parts of the world. Centella eriantha is often found in wetlands and is known to thrive in areas with full sun to partial shade. It is a perennial herb that grows up to a certain height and has distinctive white or purple flowers. The classification of Centella eriantha as a species within the genus Centella is further supported by its unique characteristics and morphological features.

intro: Centella eriantha, also known as Indian pennywort or gotu kola, is a perennial aquatic plant native to Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to promote overall health and well-being, with reported health benefits including improved cognitive function, enhanced wound healing, and reduced inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Centella eriantha include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Centella eriantha is often prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or capsule, and is also used in topical creams and ointments to support skin health and tissue repair.
